title: Private and Final Methods not Accessing Instance Data Should Be Static
description: Non-overridable methods (private or final) that don’t access instance data can be static to prevent any misunderstanding about the method's contract. This rule excludes the following three methods when java.io.Serializable is implemented.
recommendation: Ensure that non-overridable methods (private or final) that don’t access instance data are declared as static.
impact: Avoids confusion about the method's contract and improves code readability and maintainability.

checks.message: Ensure that non-overridable methods (private or final) that don’t access instance data are declared as static.
